issues.title: [Epic] GKE Workflow
issues.body:
<!-- johnny-johnny
id: gke-workflow
schema: backlog-v1
type: epic
-->

Implement Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) as a first-class deployment target within StyxCD. The workflow should support provisioning, deployment, validation, rollback, and future promotion strategies while remaining consistent with the existing execution-plan architecture.

issues.title: Implement GKE deployment stage
issues.body:
<!-- johnny-johnny
id: implement-gke-deployment-stage
schema: backlog-v1
type: issue
parent: gke-workflow
-->

Description:

Replace the current hello-world GKE stage with a real implementation that executes gcloud and kubectl commands. The stage should deploy an application to a target GKE cluster using workflow configuration provided by the orchestrator.

issues.title: Define GKE Authentication Strategy
issues.body:
<!-- johnny-johnny
id: define-gke-authentication-strategy
schema: backlog-v1
type: issue
parent: gke-workflow
-->

Description:

Determine how StyxCD authenticates to GKE clusters. Evaluate service accounts, workload identity, and credential management approaches.

issues.body:
<!-- johnny-johnny
id: deploy-sample-application-to-gke
schema: backlog-v1
type: issue
parent: gke-workflow
-->

Deploy a sample application to GKE using StyxCD to validate the complete workflow from YAML submission through deployment execution.

issues.title: [Epic] Platform Security
issues.body:
<!-- johnny-johnny
id: platform-security
schema: backlog-v1
type: epic
-->

Implement the security foundation for StyxCD. Define how users, services, execution engines, and external systems authenticate and authorize actions within the platform. The design should support both a single-user deployment model and future multi-user enterprise deployments.

issues.title: Evaluate Control Plane Authentication Strategy
issues.body:
<!-- johnny-johnny
id: evaluate-control-plane-authentication-strategy
schema: backlog-v1
type: issue
parent: platform-security
-->

Determine how users authenticate to the StyxCD control plane. Evaluate API keys, OAuth2, OIDC, and other approaches that support both personal and enterprise deployments.

issues.title: Implement Control Plane Authentication
issues.body:
<!-- johnny-johnny
id: implement-control-plane-authentication
schema: backlog-v1
type: issue
parent: platform-security
-->

Implement authentication for StyxCD APIs and UI access based on the selected authentication strategy. The solution should support both personal deployments and future enterprise deployments.

issues.title: Define Service-to-Service Authentication Model
issues.body:
<!-- johnny-johnny
id: define-service-to-service-authentication-model
schema: backlog-v1
type: issue
parent: platform-security
-->

Define how StyxCD services authenticate with one another, including orchestrator, Jenkins, future execution engines, agents, and external integrations.

issues.body:
<!-- johnny-johnny

<!-- johnny-johnny

Define how permissions are granted within StyxCD and establish a role-based access control model that can evolve with future multi-user and enterprise requirements.

Define how workflows reference credentials without exposing sensitive information. Workflows should consume credentials through secure references rather than embedded values.

-->

Document the overall security architecture of StyxCD including authentication, authorization, secrets management, trust boundaries, audit logging, and future enterprise considerations.

issues.title: [Epic] Kharon V1 - AI Knowledge Assistant
issues.body:
<!-- johnny-johnny
id: kharon-v1-ai-knowledge-assistant
schema: backlog-v1
type: epic
-->

## Goal

Build the first production-quality version of **Kharon**, the AI knowledge assistant for StyxCD.

Kharon is an independent Python service responsible for retrieval, reasoning,
knowledge ingestion, and AI interactions. StyxCD remains the deterministic
deployment platform while Kharon provides AI-assisted capabilities.

**Guiding Principle**

> StyxCD authorizes.  
> Kharon advises.

---

# Success Criteria

- Standalone Python service
- Chat endpoint operational
- RAG implemented using StyxCD documentation
- PostgreSQL + pgvector knowledge store
- LangChain and LangGraph integrated
- Able to answer StyxCD-specific questions using retrieved documentation

---

issues.body:
<!-- johnny-johnny
id: implement-ui-parity-command-using-ferry-verb
schema: backlog-v1
type: issue
parent: styxcd-command-line-tool
-->

Create the initial StyxCD CLI command that provides parity with the current Next.js operations UI. The command should submit workflow YAML to the orchestrator and return execution metadata including execution ID, status, Jenkins links, Grafana links, and future execution details.

issues.title: [Epic] Backlog-as-Code Synchronization
issues.body:
<!-- johnny-johnny
id: backlog-as-code-synchronization
schema: backlog-v1
type: epic
-->

Create a portable project-management synchronization engine that treats

GitHub Projects as a planning interface while maintaining a

provider-independent Source of Truth (SSOT).

The synchronization engine must support bidirectional synchronization

between GitHub and the canonical backlog model while preserving

portability across future providers such as Jira, Bitbucket, Azure

DevOps, or Linear.

issues.title: Design Canonical Backlog Model
issues.body:
<!-- johnny-johnny
id: design-canonical-backlog-model
schema: backlog-v1
type: issue
parent: backlog-as-code-synchronization
-->

-   Define portable backlog.yml schema.

-   Support epics, stories, acceptance criteria, provider metadata.

issues.title: Implement GitHub Project Export
issues.body:
<!-- johnny-johnny
id: implement-github-project-export
schema: backlog-v1
type: issue
parent: backlog-as-code-synchronization
-->

-   Read GitHub Projects, Issues, labels, milestones, priorities,

estimates, relationships.

issues.body:
<!-- johnny-johnny
id: generate-canonical-backlog-yml
schema: backlog-v1

parent: backlog-as-code-synchronization
-->

-   Idempotent generation.

-   Provider metadata isolated.

issues.title: Dogfood Backlog-as-Code Synchronization
issues.body:
